extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe won from a two-man breakaway, outsprinting POLITT Nils in the velodrome after LAMPAERT Yves closed to 18 seconds too late.
tactical_patterns: GILBERT Philippe attacked early with POLITT Nils, shed domestique KREDER Wesley through sustained pressure before SAGAN arrived
VAN AERT Wout's repeated mechanical/crash setbacks after chasing solo into the winning move destroyed his contention despite courage
LAMPAERT Yves rode tempo from chasers, closing 40s to 18s once inside velodrome but missed podium timing
SAGAN Peter forced to chase from 25s deficit after OSS Daniel crash removed key support before GILBERT's move

rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: won despite pundits deeming Perry-Roubaix unsuited to him, proving sustained power on cobbles trumps sprinting pedigree
rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: matched GILBERT's pace to velodrome but lacked tactical sprint positioning experience, forced to lead high line
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: three separate bike incidents while in winning move drained reserves completely despite heroic multi-chase effort
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: timed solo pursuit perfectly to reach velodrome 18s down, nearly stole podium from two-man break
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: lost key lieutenant OSS Daniel early, then chased 25s gap solo, fight visibly gone before finale
rider_signals.VANMARCKE Sep: neutral: in chase group led by KÜNG Stefan but faded from contention in final sectors
